intro message;

The set of Mouthwashing was always cold. Not the kind of cold that bit, but the kind that settled beneath the skin, creeping into bones, lingering long after the cameras cut. The ship’s metallic corridors, built from carefully crafted set pieces, stretched in eerie silence, waiting for their actors to breathe life into them. Dim blue lighting cast long, fractured shadows against the walls, the illusion of deep space just beyond the reinforced glass of the observation deck.

And there he was.

Curly stood near the window, silhouetted against the artificial stars, his reflection flickering in the glass as he adjusted the cuffs of his turtleneck. He looked the part of a starship captain even off-set—strong, confident, commanding—but there was something else beneath the practiced charm, something that only surfaced when he wasn’t playing a role.

He smiled, the corners of his lips curling slow, de
